content:
## When the Notice Window Applies
Use this liability standard for debit-card and electronic-fund-transfer error reports tied to these categories: unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud. The customer must give notice no later than 60 days after the statement showing the questioned transaction was made available.

If the report is about an error that is not unauthorized, customer liability is 0. Do not apply the unauthorized-transaction caps to non-unauthorized error types.

## Customer Liability Caps
- Reported within 2 business days after discovery: maximum liability is 50.
- Reported after that period but still within the statement notice window: maximum liability is 500.
- Reported after the statement notice window: liability is unlimited liability in this benchmark policy; the encoded cap is -1.

## Handling Notes
Confirm the customer’s discovery date and the statement date before quoting a liability cap. If either date is missing, ask for it before classifying the report. For intake documentation, see "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements". For provisional-credit timing, see "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy".
title: Debit Card Error Notice and Liability
